decks.notes: This deck focuses on using a diverse array of creatures and spells to set up powerful combo plays. By using cards like Chthonian Nightmare and Flare of Malice to control the opponent's board, Thoughtseize to disrupt their hand, and Phoenix Fleet Airship for aggressive flying pressure, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent quickly. The inclusion of Gravecrawler and Shambling Ghast allows for recurring threats, while Orcish Bowmasters and Marionette Apprentice provide additional utility. With the right setup and proper sequencing, this deck can achieve explosive turns and secure victory.

This deck focuses on quickly getting powerful creatures into play by utilizing instant reanimation spells like Goryo's Vengeance and Faithful Mending. The goal is to set up a combo where Atraxa, Grand Unifier can be brought back to the battlefield multiple times in one turn, creating a overwhelming board presence. The inclusion of powerful creatures like Griselbrand and Psychic Frog, along with disruptive spells like Thoughtseize and Force of Negation, helps to control the game and protect the combo. Additionally, cards like Ephemerate and Prismatic Ending provide versatility and flexibility in different situations.

The main concept of this deck is to generate mana quickly using rituals and medallions to cast multiple spells in a single turn, while using Ral, Monsoon Mage to deal damage to the opponent. The deck also utilizes Past in Flames to recur spells from the graveyard and Glimpse the Impossible to draw additional cards. The goal is to set up a big combo turn where you can chain multiple spells together to overwhelm the opponent and finish them off with a Grapeshot. The deck aims to be fast and efficient in order to win before the opponent can establish their own game plan.

The key strategy of this UW Control deck is to maintain control of the game through a combination of counterspells, removal spells, and card advantage engines. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan with cards like Force of Negation, Spell Snare, and Teferi, Time Raveler, while also generating card advantage with Narset, Parter of Veils and Consult the Star Charts. The deck also includes powerful board wipes like Supreme Verdict and Wrath of the Skies to deal with threats on the battlefield. Overall, the deck seeks to outlast the opponent and win through incremental advantage.

This deck revolves around utilizing Allosaurus Rider's ability to cheat out Ghalta, Stampede Tyrant by discarding it with Neoform or Eldritch Evolution. By casting Allosaurus Rider for free with its alternate casting cost, you can easily put Ghalta into play for a massive, game-winning swing. The deck also includes various protection spells such as Pact of Negation and Veil of Summer to ensure your combo resolves successfully. Additionally, cards like Consign to Memory and Disciple of Freyalise help to generate additional value and resources to support the combo strategy.

The main concept of this deck is to cycle through cards quickly using cards like Street Wraith and Shardless Agent to set up for a big finish with Living End, which allows you to return all creature cards from your graveyard to the battlefield. The deck also includes a variety of creatures with powerful abilities to control the board and disrupt your opponent's strategy. By combining efficient cycling with powerful creatures, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent and secure a quick victory.

The main focus of this deck is to control the game by assembling the UrzaTron lands (Urza's Tower, Urza's Power Plant, Urza's Mine) to generate a large amount of mana. This mana is then used to cast powerful Eldrazi creatures like Emrakul, the Promised End and Thought-Knot Seer, while also utilizing cards like Karn, the Great Creator and Ugin, the Eye of the Storms for additional control and removal. Chalice of the Void and Glaring Fleshraker help disrupt the opponent's game plan, making it difficult for them to counter your powerful plays.

This deck focuses on assembling the UrzaTron land combo to generate massive amounts of mana and control the game with powerful Eldrazi creatures and colorless spells. The key strategy is to quickly ramp up mana production using Urza's Mine, Power Plant, and Tower, allowing for early plays of Karn, Ugin, and Thought-Knot Seer to disrupt the opponent's plans. Utilizing Chalice of the Void to shut down opposing spells and All Is Dust to clear the board, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with powerful threats and efficient removal.

The deck focuses on quickly filling up the graveyard with powerful creatures like Ulamog, the Defiler, Griselbrand, and Atraxa, Grand Unifier, and then using Goryo's Vengeance to cheat them into play. Additional support cards like Thoughtseize, Fatal Push, and Consign to Memory help control the board and disrupt the opponent's strategy, while Faithless Looting helps dig for combo pieces. The deck aims to quickly reanimate big threats and overwhelm the opponent before they can set up their own game plan.

The deck focuses on quickly building a strong board presence with aggressive creatures and efficient removal spells. It utilizes cards like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah, Guide of Souls, and Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer to apply pressure on the opponent. The inclusion of cards like Goblin Bombardment and Chthonian Nightmare allows for additional reach and disruption. The combination of creatures and direct damage spells makes it a versatile and adaptable deck able to handle a variety of threats from the opponent. Overall, the deck aims to swarm the board and take control of the game early on.

The deck focuses on using ramp spells and Eldrazi creatures to quickly build up mana and overwhelm the opponent with powerful threats. It also includes control elements to disrupt the opponent's strategy, such as Fight Rigging and Malevolent Rumble. Emrakul, the Promised End and Ugin, Eye of the Storms provide strong finishers, while cards like Disciple of Freyalise and Sowing Mycospawn help accelerate the deck's game plan. The deck aims to control the board early on and then bring out massive Eldrazi creatures to close out the game.

The deck focuses on combining land ramp, utility lands, and creatures with powerful abilities to quickly assemble the Amulet of Vigor and Primeval Titan combo. By using cards like Explore and Arboreal Grazer to put additional lands into play, the deck can quickly generate massive amounts of mana and search for key lands such as Slayers' Stronghold and Boros Garrison. Once Primeval Titan enters the battlefield, the deck can search for multiple utility lands and punch through for lethal damage with double strike and haste.

The deck focuses on utilizing mill cards to rapidly deplete the opponent's library, while also controlling the board with removal spells and counterspells. Cards like Ruin Crab, Hedron Crab, and Tasha's Hideous Laughter are key to the overall strategy, while Archive Trap can quickly mill a large portion of the opponent's library if played at the right time. By combining mill effects with disruption spells like Drown in the Loch and Fatal Push, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan and mill them out before they can execute their win condition.

The focus of this deck is to control the game by utilizing the UrzaTron lands to ramp up to powerful Eldrazi creatures and Ugin planeswalkers. With cards like Karn, the Great Creator and Chalice of the Void to disrupt opponents, the deck aims to establish board presence with Devourer of Destiny and Thought-Knot Seer while also having removal options like Dismember. The deck's win condition is to overwhelm the opponent with powerful threats such as Emrakul, the Promised End and Ugin, Eye of the Storms, while disrupting their game plan.

The deck focuses on using the Amulet of Vigor to quickly ramp up mana and accelerate into playing Primeval Titan to tutor for key lands like Gruul Turf and Simic Growth Chamber. The deck aims to combo off by playing cards like Scapeshift or Aftermath Analyst to generate massive amounts of mana and pull off explosive plays, potentially winning the game in one turn. The deck also includes toolbox cards like Green Sun's Zenith to search for specific creatures or lands to further enhance its combo potential.

The deck focuses on assembling a powerful combo using cards such as Blade of the Bloodchief, Basking Broodscale, and Glaring Fleshraker, along with support cards like Eldrazi Temple and Urza's Saga. By utilizing creature sacrifice effects and generating a large number of tokens, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a swarm of powerful creatures and disruptive spells. The inclusion of Emrakul, the Promised End as a finisher adds an extra layer of threat to the deck, making it a formidable force in Modern 1 vs 1 matchups.
